symbols.name: \(n\)
symbols.def: Number of fixed experimental units on the cycle.

symbols.name: \(M\)
symbols.def: Radius of the local-interference neighborhood.

symbols.name: \(\delta\)
symbols.def: Fixed exponent delimiting the growing-radius regime.

symbols.name: \(\alpha\)
symbols.def: Illustrative exponent for a sublinear interference radius.

symbols.name: \(C_0\)
symbols.def: Fixed outcome envelope and upper arc-wise score-variance constant.

symbols.name: \(c_0\)
symbols.def: Fixed lower bound on arc-wise score variance per vertex.

symbols.name: \(V_n\)
symbols.def: Vertex set of the cycle with cyclic addition.

symbols.name: \(\mathsf C_n\)
symbols.def: Undirected cycle graph on \(V_n\).

symbols.name: \(\mathcal N_i\)
symbols.def: Neighborhood \(\mathcal N_i=\{j\in V_n:d_{\mathsf C_n}(i,j)\le M\}\).

symbols.name: \(W\)
symbols.def: Bernoulli treatment assignment vector.

symbols.name: \(w\)
symbols.def: Generic deterministic assignment vector used to index potential outcomes.

symbols.name: \(w'\)
symbols.def: Second generic deterministic assignment vector used to state local interference.

symbols.name: \(Y_i(w)\)
symbols.def: Fixed potential outcome of unit \(i\) under assignment \(w\).

symbols.name: \(Y\)
symbols.def: Collection \(Y=\{Y_i(w):i\in V_n,w\in\{0,1\}^{V_n}\}\).

symbols.name: \(\tau\)
symbols.def: Direct-effect contrast held constant across units and neighbor assignments.

symbols.name: \(\psi_i\)
symbols.def: Direct-effect score \(\psi_i=2(2W_i-1)Y_i(W)\).

symbols.name: \(\widehat\tau\)
symbols.def: Estimator \(\widehat\tau=n^{-1}\sum_{i\in V_n}\psi_i\).

symbols.name: \(\mathcal A_{n,M}\)
symbols.def: Cyclic contiguous arcs \(I\subseteq V_n\) with \(|I|\ge 2M+1\).

symbols.name: \(I\)
symbols.def: Generic cyclic arc over which the score-variance density is evaluated.

symbols.name: \(a\)
symbols.def: Baseline coefficient in the explicit local-response family verifying the arc-variance condition.

symbols.name: \(\eta\)
symbols.def: Nearest-neighbor coefficient in the explicit local-response family verifying the arc-variance condition.

symbols.name: \(S\)
symbols.def: Random index set whose treatment coordinates are Gibbs-rerandomized.

symbols.name: \(\mu\)
symbols.def: Assignment-independent law of \(S\).

symbols.name: \(D(S)\)
symbols.def: Set \(D(S)=\{i\in V_n:\mathcal N_i\cap S\ne\varnothing\}\).

symbols.name: \(g\)
symbols.def: One fixed measurable map of \(S\), \(W_{-S}\), and \(\{Y_i(W):i\notin D(S)\}\).

symbols.name: \(\lambda_\mu\)
symbols.def: Spectral gap of the Bernoulli Gibbs-rerandomization transition induced by \(\mu\).

symbols.name: \(\mathcal G\)
symbols.space: \(\{g:g\text{ is an observable measurable proxy map}\}\)
symbols.def: Measurable-map space used in the recompute rule class.

symbols.name: \(\mathfrak R_n\)
symbols.def: Class of assignment-independent positive-gap update laws paired with schedule-uniform observable proxies.

symbols.name: \(L_\star\)
symbols.def: Canonical block scale \(L_\star=\lfloor\sqrt{Mn}\rfloor\).

symbols.name: \(L\)
symbols.def: Block length in the no-interference calibration.

symbols.name: \(\mu_L,g_L\)
symbols.def: Uniform length-\(L\) block law and leave-block proxy in the no-interference calibration.

symbols.name: \(\mu_{L_\star},g_{L_\star}\)
symbols.def: Update law and observable proxy induced by the padded uniform block at length \(L_\star\).

symbols.name: \(\widehat V_{\mu,g}\)
symbols.def: Recompute variance estimator normalized by \(\lambda_\mu\).

symbols.name: \(\mathcal Y_n(M;c_0,C_0)\)
symbols.def: Bounded constant-direct-effect local-interference schedules with nondegenerate arc variances.

symbols.name: \(R_n^\star(M)\)
symbols.def: Smallest worst-case expected variance-inflation ratio over \(\mathfrak R_n\).

symbols.name: \(C_{\mathrm{up}}\)
symbols.def: Finite constant in the padded-block upper bound, depending only on \(c_0,C_0,\delta\).

symbols.name: \(c\)
symbols.def: Putative positive constant in the all-rule lower-bound question.

symbols.name: \(\mathsf H_{n,M}\)
symbols.def: Joint geometry and missing-information lower-bound program for an arbitrary recompute rule.

novel.justification: This is a constant own-treatment-effect restriction that permits unrestricted radius-\(M\) spillovers through the neighbor assignment. It models interventions whose direct effect is operationally stable while ambient local congestion or peer exposure changes the outcome level; the explicit local schedules displayed in Assumption \(\textnormal{ass:arc-variance-density}\) satisfy it.

This finite-population stability condition retains experimental settings with persistent assignment variation on every spatial or temporal segment. For \(M\ge1\), the explicit local family \(Y_i(w)=a+\eta(2w_{i+1}-1)+\tau(w_i-1/2)\), with \(4(a^2+\eta^2)\in[c_0,C_0]\) and \(|a|+|\eta|+1/4\le C_0\), obeys the condition because \(\operatorname{Var}_W(\sum_{i\in I}\psi_i)=4(a^2+\eta^2)|I|\). For \(M=0\), take \(\eta=0\).

This sublinear-radius asymptotic regime represents spatial or switchback deployments whose spillover window grows with deployment length but remains smaller than the experimental horizon; for example, \(M=\lfloor n^\alpha\rfloor\) with \(0<\alpha<1\).

assumptions.condition: \(S\sim\mu\) is independent of \(W\) and of the fixed schedule \(Y\).

definitions.name: Cycle neighborhoods
definitions.construction: \(V_n=\mathbb Z/n\mathbb Z,\quad \mathcal N_i=\{j\in V_n:d_{\mathsf C_n}(i,j)\le M\},\quad \mathcal A_{n,M}=\{I\subseteq V_n:I\text{ is a cyclic arc and }|I|\ge2M+1\}\).

definitions.name: Regular local-interference schedules
definitions.construction: \(\mathcal Y_n(M;c_0,C_0)=\{Y\in\mathbb R^{n2^n}:Y\text{ satisfies Assumptions }\textnormal{ass:radius-local-interference},\textnormal{ ass:constant-direct-effect},\textnormal{ ass:bounded-schedule},\textnormal{ and ass:arc-variance-density for this }n\}\).

definitions.name: Outcome-blind recompute class
definitions.construction: \(\mathfrak R_n=\{(\mu,g):\mu\text{ satisfies Assumptions }\textnormal{ass:outcome-blind-update}\text{ and ass:positive-gibbs-gap};\ g: (S,W_{-S},\{Y_i(W):i\notin D(S)\})\mapsto\mathbb R\text{ is one fixed measurable map for every }Y\in\mathcal Y_n(M;c_0,C_0)\}\).

definitions.name: No-interference uniform-block rule
definitions.construction: \(M=0,\quad S=\{s,\ldots,s+L-1\}\text{ for }s\sim\operatorname{Unif}(V_n),\quad \mu_L=\mathcal L(S),\quad D(S)=S,\quad g_L=\frac{1}{n-L}\sum_{i\notin S}\psi_i,\quad\lambda_{\mu_L}=L/n\).

definitions.name: Padded uniform-block rule
definitions.construction: \(L_\star=\lfloor\sqrt{Mn}\rfloor,\quad S=\{s,\ldots,s+L_\star-1\}\text{ for }s\sim\operatorname{Unif}(V_n),\quad\mu_{L_\star}=\mathcal L(S),\quad D(S)=\{i:d_{\mathsf C_n}(i,S)\le M\},\quad g_{L_\star}=\frac{1}{n-|D(S)|}\sum_{i\notin D(S)}\psi_i,\quad\lambda_{\mu_{L_\star}}=L_\star/n\).

definitions.name: All-rule converse handle
definitions.construction: \(\mathsf H_{n,M}\) is the joint lower-bound program that maps an arbitrary \(\mu\) to its cycle-neighborhood deletion geometry and maps the hidden outcomes in \(D(S)\) to a nuisance-rich Le Cam modulus for the observable sigma-field \(\sigma(S,W_{-S},\{Y_i(W):i\notin D(S)\})\).

statements.statement: For every \(Y\in\mathcal Y_n(0;c_0,C_0)\) and \(L\in\{1,\ldots,n-1\}\), the pair \((\mu_L,g_L)\) in \(\textnormal{def:sutva-block-rule}\) belongs to \(\mathfrak R_n\) and satisfies \[\frac{\mathbb E_W[\widehat V_{\mu_L,g_L}]}{\operatorname{Var}_W(\widehat\tau)}=\frac{n}{n-L}.\] In particular, \(L=1\) yields the classical leave-one-out inflation \(n/(n-1)\).
statements.justification: The arc-variance condition makes the denominator positive, and the exact no-interference identity calibrates the proxy-loss term against the classical jackknife.
statements.gap: Kunsch1989BlockJackknife and ParkWager2026NeymanJackknife provide the block-jackknife and recomputation precedents; the displayed ratio is retained solely as the required exact reduction for this frontier.
statements.consumer: It fixes the \(L/n\) boundary term used to assess the local-interference frontier.

statements.statement: There is a constant \(C_{\mathrm{up}}<\infty\), depending only on \(c_0,C_0,\delta\), such that, for all sufficiently large \(n\) in Assumption \(\textnormal{ass:growing-radius-regime}\), the padded uniform-block rule in \(\textnormal{def:balanced-block-rule}\) belongs to \(\mathfrak R_n\) and satisfies \[\sup_{Y\in\mathcal Y_n(M;c_0,C_0)}\frac{\mathbb E_W[\widehat V_{\mu_{L_\star},g_{L_\star}}]}{\operatorname{Var}_W(\widehat\tau)}\le 1+C_{\mathrm{up}}\sqrt{M/n}.\]
statements.justification: It turns the displayed oracle-versus-proxy tradeoff into a uniform finite-population upper bound at the balanced scale.
statements.gap: ParkWager2026NeymanJackknife derives the fixed-block estimator and its scaled Newey--West form, while its cycle evidence tunes \(L\) numerically and does not give a schedule-uniform \(\sqrt{M/n}\) inflation theorem.
statements.consumer: It supplies a non-oracle, exposure-free tuning rule for randomized spatial and switchback experiments with a known interference radius.

statements.statement: Does there exist a constant \(c=c(c_0,C_0,\delta)>0\) such that, for all sufficiently large \(n\) in Assumption \(\textnormal{ass:growing-radius-regime}\), \[R_n^\star(M)\ge 1+c\sqrt{M/n}?\]
statements.justification: This is the missing minimax assertion: optimizing one block construction cannot rule out an irregular update law or a proxy that exploits the additive restriction.
statements.gap: ParkWager2026NeymanJackknife proves conservativeness and a block tradeoff for fixed rules; HarshawMiddletonSavje2026OptimizedVariance characterizes undominated quadratic bounds. Neither supplies a magnitude-sharp lower bound over arbitrary update laws and measurable recompute proxies.
statements.consumer: A positive answer certifies that block deletion at \(L_\star\) is rate-optimal for a whole class of exposure-agnostic conservative variance procedures.

sampling_model.units: The \(n\)-unit potential-outcome schedule is fixed; no superpopulation sampling is used.
sampling_model.overlap: The Bernoulli design has treatment probability \(1/2\) at every vertex.
sampling_model.nuisance_estimation: The proxy observes only \(S,W_{-S}\), and outcomes outside \(D(S)\); it is held fixed across \(\mathcal Y_n(M;c_0,C_0)\).

tldr: The focal object is the minimax expected inflation of conservative recompute variance estimation on a cycle with radius-\(M\) interference. A padded uniform update block of length \(L_\star\asymp\sqrt{Mn}\) should have excess inflation of order \(\sqrt{M/n}\). The unresolved contribution is an all-rule converse showing that neither disconnected nor multiscale update laws, nor any measurable proxy using the retained outcomes, can improve that order over the stated finite-population class.

project_justification.gap: Existing recompute theory proves expected conservativeness for a fixed update rule and displays an oracle-proxy tradeoff, but it does not quantify the unavoidable price of conservativeness after optimizing over update laws and proxies.
project_justification.niche: The constant-direct-effect class removes the ordinary Neyman heterogeneity floor, so the frontier isolates conservativeness created by recomputation under local interference.
project_justification.fill: The proposed upper theorem analyzes the anchor's padded block rule uniformly, while the open all-rule converse targets the geometric and missing-information obstruction that a single-rule optimization cannot address.

related_work: Park and Wager (2026) introduce the Neyman Jackknife, derive the fixed-block cycle construction, and identify the oracle-proxy tradeoff; their discussion explicitly treats choice of the block length as unresolved. Harshaw, Middleton, and S\u00e4vje (2026) characterize admissible quadratic conservative bounds under specified exposure mappings, but their Loewner frontier neither indexes local radius nor measures unavoidable inflation. Lin and Ding (2025) establish design-based normality and pointwise HAC conservativeness for time-series experiments, not minimax tuning or an all-rule converse. Gao and Ding (2025) construct conservative network-HAC adjustments without a sharp bandwidth lower bound. Polyanskiy and Wu (2026) develop the general dual Le Cam theory that motivates a nuisance-rich functional lower bound rather than a parametric two-point witness. K\u00fcnsch (1989) supplies the block-jackknife precedent for the contiguous-block half of the argument.
technical_internal_limitation: The all-rule lower bound may fail if the constant-direct-effect restriction permits an observable proxy to impute deleted outcomes more accurately than the deletion modulus predicts, or if a multiscale update law separates spectral-gap improvement from deletion cost. These are diagnostic failure modes for the open question, not claims about published estimators.
honest_scope: The fixed-block construction and its Poincar\u00e9 conservativeness are settled by Park and Wager. The uniform \(\sqrt{M/n}\) upper bound is proposed here as a theorem. The all-rule lower bound, including its required nuisance-rich indistinguishability construction, remains open. At \(M=0\), the stated leave-one-out calibration is the required classical reduction.

bibliography.citation: Park, B. and Wager, S. (2026). Neyman Jackknife: Design-Based Variance Estimation for Causal Inference under Interference. arXiv:2604.24017.
Harshaw, C., Middleton, J. A., and S\u00e4vje, F. (2026). Optimized Variance Estimation under Interference and Complex Experimental Designs. Journal of the American Statistical Association.
Lin, Z. and Ding, P. (2025). Unifying Regression-Based and Design-Based Causal Inference in Time-Series Experiments. arXiv:2510.22864.
Gao, M. and Ding, P. (2025). Causal Inference in Network Experiments: Regression-Based Analysis and Design-Based Properties. Journal of Econometrics 252, 106119.
Polyanskiy, Y. and Wu, Y. (2026). Dualizing Le Cam's Method for Functional Estimation I: General Theory. Annals of Statistics.
K\u00fcnsch, H. R. (1989). The Jackknife and the Bootstrap for General Stationary Observations. Annals of Statistics 17, 1217--1241.
Aronow, P. M. and Samii, C. (2017). Estimating Average Causal Effects under General Interference, with Application to a Social Network Experiment. Annals of Applied Statistics 11, 1912--1947.

comparator_promise_table.comparator_claim: A fixed update law and an observable proxy yield a variance estimator conservative in expectation; on a cycle a uniform block gives a scaled Newey--West estimator.
Admissible conservative quadratic variance bounds can be characterized and optimized in a convex program under the stated exposure structure.
Regression estimators are asymptotically normal and HAC estimators asymptotically conservative in design-based time-series experiments.
A positive-semidefinite network covariance adjustment restores asymptotic conservativeness under network interference.

seeds: A sharp all-rule recompute-inflation frontier on a locally interfering cycle: the best conservative rule has excess order \(\sqrt{M/n}\).
A cycle-isoperimetric oracle frontier: update-set geometry alone forces an \(M/L\) inflation cost at effective update scale \(L\).
A deleted-outcome modulus: every schedule-uniform observable proxy incurs an \(L/n\) loss under nuisance-rich constant-direct-effect schedules.
A uniformly valid Wald-coverage theorem for a tuned padded-block Neyman Jackknife under growing local interference.
A minimax bandwidth frontier under approximate neighborhood interference balancing decay truncation and recompute inflation.
A fractional-chromatic lower bound for design-based confidence length on genuinely overlapping exposure graphs.
A minimal-conservativeness characterization comparing completion envelopes and recompute rules for ego-cluster exposure contrasts.
An exact \(M=0\) characterization of which assignment-independent update laws reproduce the classical jackknife frontier.
A data-independent multiscale update design with an optimality certificate for conservative variance under graph interference.

seed_details.one_line: A sharp all-rule recompute-inflation frontier on a locally interfering cycle: the best conservative rule has excess order \(\sqrt{M/n}\).

anchor_paper.author: Park and Wager
anchor_paper.claim: The Neyman Jackknife gives expected conservative variance estimates from an update law and an observable recomputation proxy.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: M11+M7, not routine block optimization: the lower bound ranges over every update law and proxy.
seed_details.referee_objection: Immediate from optimizing the anchor's block prefactor.
seed_details.rebuttal: The claimed converse must exclude disconnected and multiscale update laws and nuisance-adaptive proxies, which the prefactor does not represent.

adversarial_referee.instance: A single periodic schedule cannot lower-bound every proxy.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: The question requires a nuisance-rich indistinguishable family inside the stated schedule class.

adversarial_referee.instance: Park and Wager already optimize the tradeoff.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: They establish fixed-rule formulas and numerical tuning, not an infimum over all laws and proxies.

adversarial_referee.instance: The update law and proxy class is underspecified.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: The core defines one schedule-uniform measurable proxy and an assignment-independent positive-gap update law.

seed_details.one_line: A cycle-isoperimetric oracle frontier: update-set geometry alone forces an \(M/L\) inflation cost at effective update scale \(L\).

anchor_paper.author: Park and Wager
anchor_paper.claim: Lemma 11 expresses oracle inflation through Fourier support and update-set intersection probabilities.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: M11 component, not a full frontier without a proxy half.
seed_details.referee_objection: It is just Lemma 11 with intervals substituted.
seed_details.rebuttal: A sharp isoperimetric lower bound over arbitrary random subsets is absent, but alone it is not a complete paper.

adversarial_referee.instance: Geometry is an intermediate lemma, not a causal kernel.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Retained only as a component of Seed 1.

adversarial_referee.instance: Fourier formula is already published.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: The all-law extremal geometry is new, but insufficient alone.

adversarial_referee.instance: One Fourier mode avoids genericity.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Would require a uniform schedule region; otherwise it is dropped as standalone.

seed_details.one_line: A deleted-outcome modulus: every schedule-uniform observable proxy incurs an \(L/n\) loss under nuisance-rich constant-direct-effect schedules.

anchor_paper.author: Park and Wager
anchor_paper.claim: The proxy sees outcomes only outside the deletion set and larger update sets make approximation more difficult.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: M11 lower-bound component with a Le Cam functional modulus, not a bare missing-data analogy.
seed_details.referee_objection: Constant direct effects reveal the deleted mean from retained units.
seed_details.rebuttal: Only a nuisance-rich family that agrees on retained observations can establish the required modulus; this is why it is retained as a candidate rather than asserted.

adversarial_referee.instance: A sample-mean proxy is not minimax among measurable maps.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: The proposed modulus quantifies over all measurable maps.

adversarial_referee.instance: A two-point parametric tau family is distinguishable.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: The handle explicitly requires nuisance-rich alternatives.

adversarial_referee.instance: The observation channel is not formalized.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: The sigma-field in the converse handle fixes the channel.

seed_details.one_line: A uniformly valid Wald-coverage theorem for a tuned padded-block Neyman Jackknife under growing local interference.

anchor_paper.author: Park and Wager
anchor_paper.claim: The framework guarantees conservativeness in expectation and asks when conservativeness in probability holds after choosing a block length.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: M12, not an assumed CLT: it needs a dependency-graph numerator bound and lower-tail variance control.
seed_details.referee_objection: Expectation conservativeness does not imply coverage.
seed_details.rebuttal: That is the precise unresolved lower-tail problem, but it is downstream of the sharper frontier.

adversarial_referee.instance: The anchor already gives conservative variance.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: It gives expectation-level conservativeness, not uniform studentized coverage.

adversarial_referee.instance: A numerator CLT alone proves nothing about studentization.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: A variance lower-tail theorem is separately required.

adversarial_referee.instance: Tuning selection is undefined.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Would require a fixed or split-sample tuning convention before promotion.

adversarial_referee.instance: Harshaw et al. already optimize bounds.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Only if completion and recompute classes are formally incomparable.

anchor_paper.author: Park and Wager

adversarial_referee.instance: SUTVA jackknife theory is mature.

adversarial_referee.instance: Necessity over all update laws is artificial.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Lands; not a standalone contribution.

anchor_paper.author: Park and Wager

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: M13, but the objective depends on unknown schedules and needs a minimax certificate.
seed_details.referee_objection: The design is merely a reparametrization of the update law.
seed_details.rebuttal: It survives only if a minimax saddle point or approximation ratio is established; otherwise Seed 1 is sharper.

adversarial_referee.instance: A multiscale law is asserted good without certification.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: A saddle or lower bound is mandatory.

adversarial_referee.instance: The design criterion is schedule dependent.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Use the same minimax class as Seed 1.

adversarial_referee.instance: It duplicates the all-rule frontier.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: It is subsumed unless it yields a distinct optimizer.

method_exemplars_used.adapted_technique: The Gibbs-resampling Poincare decomposition separating the oracle term from an observable recomputation proxy.
A nuisance-rich two-point modulus over the proxy observation channel rather than a parametric witness that reveals the direct effect.
Contiguous-block scaling as the constructive upper-bound benchmark.

literature_map: Park and Wager (2026) formulate conservative recomputation through an update law, a Gibbs spectral gap, and an observable proxy. Their cycle example uses a uniform length-\(L\) block and a padded deleted set, yielding a scaled Newey--West estimator. Their Fourier formula expresses oracle inflation as an update-set intersection probability divided by the spectral gap, and their monotonicity result only treats contiguous blocks. Their proxy section states that larger update sets delete more outcomes and make approximation harder. Their discussion calls selection of \(L\) and conservativeness in probability open. Harshaw, Middleton, and S\u00e4vje (2026) study admissibility among quadratic variance bounds, which is qualitative and exposure-model dependent rather than a radius-indexed inflation rate. Lin and Ding (2025) prove pointwise design-based HAC conservativeness and asymptotic normality for time-series regressions. Gao and Ding (2025) obtain conservative network-HAC adjustment but not minimax bandwidth choice. K\u00fcnsch (1989) is the moving-block jackknife ancestor. Polyanskiy and Wu (2026) provide a dual Le Cam route for a functional lower bound with rich nuisance variation. No located result proves a lower bound over all assignment-independent update laws and all schedule-uniform observable proxies.
prior_work_summary: The prior effective-exposure proposal identifies the need for a lower-bound construction on genuinely overlapping graphs, rather than disjoint clique blocks. The prior ego-cluster proposal separates completion sharpness by definition from estimator-class optimality. The accepted bipartite-design proposal establishes that design-based finite-population minimax constructions can be viable but does not contain this recomputation frontier. No prior proposal states the Park--Wager all-update-law \(\sqrt{M/n}\) converse.

novelty_justification.repo_axis: The nearest in-repository lines are the downgraded effective-exposure lower-bound proposal and the ego-cluster completion-envelope proposal. The former concerns information loss from overlapping exposures but does not define or optimize the Neyman-Jackknife recompute class. The latter asks which observable quadratic envelope is minimal, rather than quantifying the excess inflation of assignment-deletion recomputation. Neither contains \(R_n^\star(M)\), an all-update-law lower bound, or the cycle geometry-proxy tradeoff.
novelty_justification.published_axis: ParkWager2026NeymanJackknife is the closest published comparator: it proves expected conservativeness for any fixed admissible pair and analyzes a uniform block, but it does not take an infimum over update laws and measurable proxies or prove an optimal inflation rate. HarshawMiddletonSavje2026OptimizedVariance is adjacent because it studies minimal conservativeness, but its quadratic Loewner-admissibility frontier has no local-radius rate, no all-rule proxy class, and no magnitude lower bound. The precise proposed advance is a minimax expected-inflation frontier, not a reformulation of either procedure.

literature_checklist.author: Park and Wager
literature_checklist.one_line: Defines the recompute class, proves expectation conservativeness, and gives the cycle block construction and oracle-proxy decomposition.

literature_checklist.author: Harshaw, Middleton, and S\u00e4vje
literature_checklist.venue: Journal of the American Statistical Association
literature_checklist.one_line: Characterizes admissible quadratic conservative bounds but not a radius-indexed minimax inflation rate.

literature_checklist.author: Polyanskiy and Wu
literature_checklist.one_line: Dualizes Le Cam lower bounds for functional estimation and motivates the nuisance-rich proxy modulus.

literature_checklist.author: K\u00fcnsch
literature_checklist.one_line: Provides the dependent-data block-jackknife ancestor for contiguous update blocks.

literature_checklist.author: Lin and Ding
literature_checklist.one_line: Proves design-based normality and pointwise HAC conservativeness for time-series experiments.

literature_checklist.author: Gao and Ding
literature_checklist.one_line: Constructs conservative network covariance adjustment without an optimal bandwidth converse.
